title: marsdenia tonkinensis
intro: Marsdenia tonkinensis, also known as Tonkin vine or Chinese milk vine, is a perennial climbing plant native to the tropical forests of Asia, characterized by its woody stem and large, white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antispasmodic and anti-anxiety agent. The health benefits of Marsdenia tonkinensis include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of insomnia and muscle spasms. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are typically dried and used in teas, tinctures, or capsules to harness its medicinal properties.
